plan: 1. Create UI component `src/ui/threat-model-card.ts` — export `createThreatCard(finding: ThreatFinding): HTMLElement` that renders an article.card with: severity badge (Low/Medium/High with modifier class), title heading, short description, evidence list, userImpact blurb, and category label. Also export `createThreatCardList(cards: HTMLElement[]): HTMLElement` for grid container. Follow the shadow-profile-card pattern.

2. Create styles `src/modules/threat-model/threat-model.css` — section wrapper `.threat-model`, card styles `.threat-model-card`, severity badge modifiers `--low` (success green), `--medium` (warning yellow), `--high` (danger red) using existing design tokens, grid layout (1-col mobile, 2-col 768px+), hover shadow transition, reduced-motion support.

3. Create render function `src/modules/threat-model/threat-model-module.ts` — export `renderThreatModelModule(): Promise<HTMLElement>`. Collect snapshot via `collectSnapshot()`, permissions via `checkPermissions()`, entropy via `calculateEntropyScore()`, permissionDebt via `calculatePermissionDebtScore()`, inferences via `applyInferenceRules()`. Build `ThreatInput`, call `evaluateThreatRules(input)`, map findings to cards, return section element. Handle empty state gracefully.
4. Create barrel export `src/modules/threat-model/index.ts` — re-export `renderThreatModelModule` from threat-model-module.ts.
5. Register module in `src/app/main.ts` — import and call `renderThreatModelModule()` after shadow profile rendering, append result to dashboard container.
6. Import CSS — add `threat-model.css` import to `src/styles/global.css` or import it directly in the module file (follow existing pattern).

7. Write unit tests `tests/us-028/threat-model-card.test.ts` — verify card renders severity badge, title, description, evidence, userImpact; verify card list creates container with children; verify severity modifier classes are applied correctly for each level.

8. Write integration tests `tests/us-028/threat-model-module.test.ts` — verify `renderThreatModelModule()` returns a section element, renders risk cards, shows at least 4 categories when all rules trigger, handles empty findings gracefully.
9. Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` to validate all checks pass.
testing_recommendations: Unit test each severity level (low/medium/high) renders the correct badge class and text
Unit test that createThreatCard produces correct DOM structure: article with severity badge, title h3/h4, description paragraph, evidence list, userImpact section
Unit test createThreatCardList wraps cards in a grid container div
Integration test that renderThreatModelModule returns a section.threat-model element with h2 heading
Integration test with mocked high-entropy + granted permissions + inferences input triggers all 4 rule categories, verifying at least 4 risk cards render
Integration test empty findings state renders a meaningful empty message instead of blank section
Verify CSS classes match between card component and stylesheet (no typos in severity modifier names)
Run npm run lint, npm run typecheck, npm run test before marking complete
risks: Shadow profile inferences may not be easily accessible from threat-model-module since applyInferenceRules is in a sibling module — need to verify the import path and that it accepts the same snapshot/permissions data
Shoulder-surfing rule depends on window.screen dimensions which are hard to mock in jsdom — tests may need careful setup or JSDOM overrides
If checkPermissions() rejects in the threat model module, the entire module could fail — need the same try/catch pattern used in shadow-profile-module
CSS import strategy must match the existing pattern (some modules use global.css imports, others use direct imports) — inconsistency could cause missing styles
